name: Da Yu Ling Oolong
region: Da Yu Ling, Central Mountain Range, Taiwan
terroir: Highest tea-growing elevation in Taiwan (~2200–2600m), extreme cold, persistent fog, very slow leaf growth
flavor_profile: Intensely floral with an almost perfumed orchid-and-lilac character that only extreme elevation produces. Silky mouthfeel, exceptional sweetness, and a clean finish with hints of mung bean and warm milk. Forgiving to brew — quality shines through even with imprecise parameters.

brewing.tips: Very forgiving tea — focus on not over-steeping rather than hitting exact parameters. Lower temperature (85–90°C) preserves the delicate floral top notes.
